message.content:
<deliberation>
Step-by-step plan:
1) Parse each main node (REG-013, REG-018, REG-046) and identify its explicit cross-references.
2) Pull the full text of each referenced node provided in <referenced_nodes>.
3) Compare the requirements (including references) against the entire policy chunk and look for direct contradictions, narrowings, broadenings, or other taxonomy deviations.
4) Treat any missing content in the policy as Non-Deviation per rules 4 and 4.1.
5) For any deviation, extract exact quotes from both the regulation text and the policy text to evidence the mismatch and classify it per taxonomy.

REG-013 (Art10(1)):
- Cross-reference: Art10(2) (REG-014) is provided; Art10(3)-(5) are referenced by Art10(1) but not provided, so omissions are treated as Non-Deviation per rule 4.
- Core requirement: Applies specifically to “High-risk AI systems which make use of techniques involving the training of AI models”.
- Policy states “Applies to: All AI systems.” This expands scope beyond high-risk systems. That is stricter than required (over-compliance) → Severity deviation.
- No other direct contradiction: the policy’s documentation of inputs/outputs/generalizability does not negate Art10 quality criteria; absence of “validation” dataset mention is an omission and not a deviation.

REG-018 (Art10(6)):
- Cross-reference: Art10(2) (REG-014) is provided; Art10(3)-(5) not provided → omissions treated as Non-Deviation.
- Core requirement: Applies to “high-risk AI systems not using techniques involving the training of AI models,” limiting Art10(2)-(5) to testing datasets.
- Policy again applies to “All AI systems,” enlarging scope beyond high-risk → Severity deviation.
- No direct contradiction on the “testing datasets only” nuance; the policy does not negate it; omissions remain Non-Deviation.

REG-046 (Art26(4)):
- Cross-references: Art26(1) (REG-043) and Art26(2) (REG-044) are provided.
- Core requirement: For high-risk AI systems, where the deployer controls input data, the deployer must ensure input data is relevant and sufficiently representative.
- Policy applies to “All AI systems,” expanding beyond high-risk → Severity deviation.
- The policy’s instruction to “define and document … inputs … how well they represent … include analysis of limitations” does not contradict the “ensure … relevant and sufficiently representative” obligation; it could support it, but does not explicitly reassign responsibility away from deployers. Any lack of explicit “deployer” mention is treated as omission, not a deviation.

Conclusion:
- A single Severity deviation (scope overbreadth to “All AI systems”) is identified for each of the three main nodes. No other deviations are supported by direct textual contradiction.
</deliberation>
```json
[
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-013",
    "clause": "Art10(1)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-014"],
    "deviation_found": true,
    "deviations": [
      {
        "deviation_type": "Severity deviation",
        "gdpr_quote": "High-risk AI systems which make use of techniques involving the training of AI models with data shall be developed on the basis of training, validation and testing data sets that meet the quality criteria referred to in paragraphs 2 to 5 whenever such data sets are used.",
        "policy_quote": "Applies to: All AI systems.",
        "mismatch": "The policy expands applicability from only high-risk AI systems to all AI systems.",
        "analysis": "Art10(1) applies specifically to high-risk AI systems; the policy imposes its requirement set on all AI systems, which is stricter than required. Art10(2) (REG-014) was reviewed; omissions about its details in the policy are treated as Non-Deviation."
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-018",
    "clause": "Art10(6)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-014"],
    "deviation_found": true,
    "deviations": [
      {
        "deviation_type": "Severity deviation",
        "gdpr_quote": "For the development of high-risk AI systems not using techniques involving the training of AI models, paragraphs 2 to 5 apply only to the testing data sets.",
        "policy_quote": "Applies to: All AI systems.",
        "mismatch": "The policy expands applicability from only high-risk AI systems to all AI systems.",
        "analysis": "Art10(6) limits obligations to high-risk AI systems and, in that case, only to testing datasets; the policy’s blanket scope covers all AI systems, which is stricter. Art10(2) (REG-014) was considered; lack of explicit treatment in the policy is an omission (Non-Deviation)."
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-046",
    "clause": "Art26(4)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-043", "REG-044"],
    "deviation_found": true,
    "deviations": [
      {
        "deviation_type": "Severity deviation",
        "gdpr_quote": "Without prejudice to paragraphs 1 and 2, to the extent the deployer exercises control over the input data, that deployer shall ensure that input data is relevant and sufficiently representative in view of the intended purpose of the high-risk AI system.",
        "policy_quote": "Applies to: All AI systems.",
        "mismatch": "The policy expands applicability from only high-risk AI systems to all AI systems.",
        "analysis": "Art26(4) applies to high-risk AI systems and places an obligation on the deployer; the policy applies its requirements to all AI systems, which is stricter. Art26(1) (REG-043) and Art26(2) (REG-044) were considered; the policy’s lack of explicit deployer assignment is an omission, not a contradiction."
      }
    ]
  }
]
```
